explanation: The property captures that when the inspiratory pause (which is part of the overall inspiration time) times out, the system must immediately proceed to the expiration phase. The condition "inspiratory pause timeout is over" is mapped to `inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime`, as this is the most relevant atomic proposition indicating a timeout for an inspiratory phase/event. The phrase "proceed immediately to expiration" is translated to `X expiratoryPhaseStart`, meaning the expiration phase starts in the next state. The global implication ensures this behavior holds at all times.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory pause timeout is over, the system must immediately transition to the expiration phase. 'inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime' represents the inspiratory pause timeout being over, and 'expiratoryPhaseStart' represents the start of the expiration phase. The 'X' operator ensures 'immediately' (in the next state) and 'G' ensures this holds true at all times.

The property ensures that whenever the inspiratory pause timeout occurs (i.e., the 'inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime' signal transitions from false to true), the system immediately proceeds to the expiration phase (i.e., 'expiratoryPhaseStart' becomes true in the very next time step). The second part of the conjunction also states that if the inspiratory pause timeout is already active at the initial state (time 0), then the expiration phase must also start immediately (at time 0).

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in the inspiratory pause state (bool_exp1) AND the inspiratory clock has exceeded or reached the maximum inspiratory time (timeout) (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the expiratory phase must begin (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that upon the inspiratory pause timeout, the cycle must immediately transition to expiration.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory pause is active and its duration has exceeded the set inspiratory time (indicating a timeout), the system must immediately proceed to the expiratory phase. 'inspiratoryPause & inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime' captures the condition 'inspiratory pause timeout is over', and 'X expiratoryPhaseStart' captures 'the cycle shall proceed immediately to expiration'.

The property states that globally, whenever the inspiratory pause timeout is over (represented by 'inspiratoryPause' being active and the 'inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime' indicating the inspiration phase has exceeded its allowed duration, effectively marking the end of the inspiratory pause), the system must immediately proceed to the start of the expiratory phase.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the inspiratory pause timeout (max_ins_pause) occurs, which means the inspiratory clock is greater than or equal to the inspiratory time (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the expiratory phase must immediately begin (bool_exp3). This captures the immediate transition requirement.

The property ensures that whenever the inspiratory pause timeout condition is met (meaning the inspiratory clock has exceeded or reached the maximum inspiratory time for the pause), the system must immediately transition to the expiratory phase.

The property ensures that when the inspiratory pause timeout condition (`inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime`) becomes true (i.e., it transitions from false to true), the system immediately proceeds to the expiration phase (`expiratoryPhaseStart`) in the very next state. It also covers the initial state, ensuring that if the timeout condition is already met at the start, the expiratory phase begins immediately. This accurately captures the "When X is over, then immediately Y" requirement, where the timeout being over is interpreted as the trigger event.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the inspiratory clock indicates that the inspiratory time (timeout) has been reached or exceeded (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime), it must be immediately followed in the next state (X) by the start of the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhaseStart). This directly captures the requirement for the cycle to proceed immediately to expiration once the inspiratory pause timeout is over.
